quoteLast: the Battle Cruisers turned to the Northward, and _Queen  Mary_ was again attacked by a Submarine. The attack was avoided by the  use of the helm. _Lowestoft_ was also unsuccessfully attacked. The  Battle Cruisers covered the retirement until nightfall. By 6 p.m., the  retirement having been well executed and all Destroyers accounted for,  I altered course, spread the Light Cruisers, and swept northwards in  accordance with the Commander-in-Chief’s orders. At 7.45 p.m. I  detached _Liverpool_ to Rosyth with German prisoners, seven officers  and 79 men, survivors from _Mainz_. No further incident occurred.—I
title: Submarines, Mines and Torpedoes in the War
author: Domville-Fife, Charles W. (Charles William), 1886-1960
